title: lost_encryption_keys
description: Encryption keys for critical data stores are lost or become inaccessible, threatening data availability.

timeline.title: Key Vault Access Failure
timeline.description: Applications begin failing with decryption errors. The key management service is returning errors.

timeline.title: Encrypted Data Inaccessible
timeline.description: All data encrypted with the affected keys is inaccessible. Services depending on this data go offline.

timeline.title: Key Vault Investigation
timeline.description: Engineers investigate the key management service and find it entered a sealed state after unexpected restarts.

timeline.title: Unseal Process Initiated
timeline.description: The key vault unseal procedure is initiated using distributed unseal keys held by key custodians.

timeline.title: Key Vault Unsealed
timeline.description: Three of five key custodians provide their unseal key shares. The vault unseals successfully.

timeline.title: Data Access Restored
timeline.description: Applications regain access to encryption keys. All encrypted data is accessible again.

node-1.title: Encryption Key Access Failure
node-1.situation: Applications are failing with decryption errors across all services that use data-at-rest encryption. The HashiCorp Vault cluster has entered a sealed state after multiple unexpected pod restarts in Kubernetes. All data protected by these keys is currently inaccessible.
node-1.question: What is your immediate action?

options.label: A
options.text: Declare a P1 incident, identify and contact all key custodians who hold Vault unseal key shares, verify the Vault cluster is intact (not corrupted), and initiate the formal unseal procedure.

options.label: B
options.text: Attempt to access the Vault data directory directly to extract keys without using the unseal procedure.

options.label: C
options.text: Re-initialize the Vault cluster with new keys, permanently losing access to all previously encrypted data.

node-2.title: Unseal Procedure
node-2.situation: The Vault cluster integrity is confirmed — all data is intact. The unseal procedure requires 3 of 5 key shares (Shamir secret sharing). Two key custodians are immediately available. A third is reachable by phone. The remaining two are on vacation and unreachable.
node-2.question: How do you proceed?

options.label: A
options.text: Gather the 3 available unseal key shares from the two on-site custodians and the one reachable by phone, following proper identity verification for the phone-based custodian, and submit shares to unseal the vault.

options.label: B
options.text: Wait until all 5 custodians are available before beginning the unseal procedure, prolonging the outage unnecessarily.

node-3.title: Vault Unsealed and Services Restored
node-3.situation: The Vault has been unsealed and applications are regaining access to encryption keys. However, the root cause of the Vault sealing must be addressed: the Kubernetes auto-unseal configuration was misconfigured and is not functioning. Without auto-unseal, every Vault restart will require manual unsealing.
node-3.question: What do you do?

options.label: A
options.text: Investigate and fix the Kubernetes auto-unseal configuration (e.g., AWS KMS or Azure Key Vault auto-unseal), test the auto-unseal in a staging environment, and deploy the fix to prevent future manual unseal requirements.

options.label: B
options.text: Accept the manual unseal requirement and rely on custodians being available every time Vault restarts.

node-4.title: Key Management Resilience Review
node-4.situation: Auto-unseal is now correctly configured and tested. The incident has exposed several gaps in the key management strategy: two custodians were unreachable (barely meeting quorum), and there is no documented key custodian succession plan.
node-4.question: What is your next step?

options.label: A
options.text: Conduct a key management policy review: increase the custodian pool from 5 to 7 with a 3-of-7 quorum, establish clear custodian succession and availability requirements, implement key custodian contact drills, and document the incident.

options.label: B
options.text: Close the incident after auto-unseal is fixed without addressing the custodian availability gaps.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: You handled the key management crisis by following the proper unseal procedure with available custodians, restoring service access, fixing the broken auto-unseal configuration, and improving the key custodian resilience policy to prevent future availability gaps.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
